Generalized R-learner for continuous treatment (dose-response on a grid)

Source: R/meta_learners.R
r_learner_continuous.Rd

Cross-fits nuisance regressions for \(E[Y \mid D, X]\) and \(E[D \mid X]\), then runs weighted local linear regressions of residuals on a dose_grid (Epanechnikov weights). Base learners match RLearner: "ranger", "lm", or "xgb".

Usage

r_learner_continuous(
  data,
  outcome = "Y",
  treatment = "D",
  covariates = NULL,
  dose_grid = seq(0, 6, by = 0.5),
  learner = c("ranger", "lm", "xgb"),
  n_fold = 5L,
  bandwidth = NULL,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

data

A data.frame with outcome, treatment, and covariates.

outcome

Name of the outcome column (default "Y").

treatment

Name of the continuous treatment column (default "D").

covariates

Character vector of covariate column names. If NULL, uses paste0("X", 1:10) when those columns exist, otherwise all columns except outcome and treatment.

dose_grid

Dose values at which to estimate \(\partial E[Y(d)\mid X]/\partial d\) (local linear slope in \(D\)).

learner

"ranger", "lm", or "xgb" (same spirit as RLearner).

n_fold

Number of cross-fitting folds for nuisance models.

bandwidth

Kernel half-width for local regression; default sd(D) * n^(-1/5).

seed

Optional integer; if set, fold assignment is reproducible.

...

Passed to ranger::ranger or xgboost::xgboost.

Value

A list with dose_grid, tau_grid (matrix, length(dose_grid) by \(n\)), X (covariate matrix), and cross-fitted mu_hat, e_hat.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# r_learner_continuous(...)
} # }
